diagnosis_summary: Detected semiconductor symptoms were mapped through item_master, knowledge cards, causal graph paths, and similar cases. The result is a ranked RCA candidate list, not a single confirmed root cause.

ranked_hypotheses.hypothesis: GAA short-Lg DIBL and SS increase
ranked_hypotheses.electrical_mechanism: Worse gate electrostatic control increases drain coupling, subthreshold slope, and short-channel Vth roll-off.
structural_cause: gate stack EOT or work-function variation
supporting_evidence: DIBL and SS increase together on short Lg structures
Vth roll-off worsens with Lg scaling
Inline gate/channel CD or spacer metric shifts in same lots
contradicting_evidence: Only long-Lg Vth shifts without DIBL/SS change
No matching Inline/gate CD or spacer shift
Wafer-local pattern suggests defectivity instead of global electrostatics
recommended_checks: Plot DIBL vs SS by Lg and polarity
Correlate short-Lg DIBL with gate/channel CD
Check Vth roll-off trend vs route split
Review channel release and inner spacer Inline modules

ranked_hypotheses.electrical_mechanism: Gate control degradation increases drain coupling and weakens subthreshold slope.
supporting_evidence: DIBL and SS move together
short-Lg structures are more sensitive than long-Lg
Inline NS/GATE_CD/IS_THK shift in same lots
contradicting_evidence: Only DIBL moves while SS and Vth roll-off are stable
No short-Lg dependence
Wafer-map pattern indicates local defect instead of module drift
recommended_checks: Plot DIBL vs SS by lot_wf
Compare short-Lg and long-Lg Vth roll-off
Join Inline GATE_CD/NS/IS to ET by lot_wf

ranked_hypotheses.electrical_mechanism: A physical bridge or local defect creates unintended conductive path.
supporting_evidence: localized wafer map pattern
comb/serpentine fail
inspection defect signal
contradicting_evidence: lot-wide smooth parametric shift
no spatial clustering
recommended_checks: Review wafer/reticle map
Check defect inspection at suspected layer
Compare comb/serpentine structures

ranked_hypotheses.hypothesis: Inline CD shift linked to ET metric
ranked_hypotheses.electrical_mechanism: Geometry and CD changes can modulate electrostatics, contact area, and drive current depending on layer and structure.
supporting_evidence: Inline metric and ET metric correlate by lot_wf or shot
effect direction matches physical mechanism
same module timing precedes ET measurement
contradicting_evidence: correlation disappears after lot/wafer grouping
Inline measured after ET or unrelated layer
single outlier drives fit
recommended_checks: Use shot key when both sides have coordinates
INLINE avg and ET median by lot_wf if no exact match
Show fit line and outliers
Filter by knob/split carefully

ranked_hypotheses.electrical_mechanism: Gate dielectric thinning, trap generation, or local defects increase tunneling or trap-assisted leakage.
process_root_cause: RMG thermal or metal contamination issue
supporting_evidence: IGATE increases with EOT/thickness monitor shift
leakage map has module-specific spatial signature
SS or Vth instability accompanies leakage
contradicting_evidence: Only drain leakage/IOFF changes with DIBL
No gate bias dependence
contact resistance metrics dominate Ion loss
recommended_checks: Plot IGATE by gate bias and device size
Check EOT/gate dielectric Inline
Compare IGATE with SS/Vth sigma
Review RMG and gate dielectric chambers

do_not_conclude: Do not infer raw item meaning from the item name alone.
Do not conclude a single process root cause from one metric.
Do not treat CA_RS as sheet resistance unless unit/test structure supports Rsheet.
Do not use LLM-generated SQL; only whitelisted backend tools may query data.

item.meaning: Short-channel electrostatic control metric. Higher DIBL usually means worse channel/gate control.
Subthreshold gate-control metric. Higher SS indicates degraded switching slope or interface/electrostatic issue.
Gate dielectric leakage. Higher value can indicate dielectric, RMG, plasma, or reliability issue.

candidates.meaning: CA/MOL conductive film sheet resistance only when unit and structure support Rsheet interpretation.
candidates.ambiguity_note: CA_RS must not be treated as sheet resistance if Kelvin/TLM/contact-chain structure is used.

candidates.meaning: CA contact resistance candidate from Kelvin/TLM structures.
Cumulative contact-chain resistance, sensitive to opens, CD, liner/fill and contact integrity.

resolved.ambiguity: CA_RS is ambiguous without unit/test_structure/measurement_method; do not conclude sheet resistance from the raw name alone.

item.meaning: Gate/channel length proxy. Shorter Lg can increase DIBL and Vth roll-off.
Leakage caused by bridge/short path. Needs layout, defect, and wafer map evidence.

warnings: CA_RS is ambiguous without unit/test_structure/measurement_method; do not conclude sheet resistance from the raw name alone.
interpreted_items.rule: Never infer a raw item meaning from the name alone; use item_master with unit/source_type/test_structure/layer/measurement_method.

leakage map has module-specific spatial signature

knowledge_cards.title: CA resistance increase with CA CD decrease
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Reduced contact aperture or degraded liner/fill increases CA/MOL resistance.
supporting_evidence: CA_CD decreases while CA resistance metrics increase
Kelvin/TLM or chain structures confirm contact path
Inline and ET move in same affected lots
contradicting_evidence: CA_CD stable and only sheet structure moves
Resistance change isolated to a non-contact test structure
recommended_checks: Resolve CA_RS with unit/test_structure first
Scatter CA_CD vs CA_RC/CHAIN_R
Check CA defect/open maps

knowledge_cards.title: CA resistance increase with CA CD decrease
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Smaller contact opening or degraded contact interface increases contact/chain resistance.
process_root_causes: metal fill or barrier process drift
supporting_evidence: CA_CD decreases while CA_RC_KELVIN or chain resistance increases
Contact chain fail/open increases
Affected lots share CA/MOL route or chamber
contradicting_evidence: CA_RS name is sheet-resistance structure rather than Kelvin/contact
CA_CD stable and resistance only in one device polarity
Resistance shift follows S/D epi not contact module
recommended_checks: Resolve CA_RS using unit/test_structure before RCA
Plot CA_CD vs CA_RC_KELVIN
Check chain open rate by wafer map
Review CA etch/preclean/fill splits

knowledge_cards.title: SRAM Vmin increase with variability
knowledge_cards.electrical_mechanism: Vmin is sensitive to transistor strength mismatch, leakage, Vth sigma, and local defect/fail signatures.
structural_causes: Vth sigma or leakage tail increase
supporting_evidence: SRAM Vmin tail increases with Vth sigma or Ion sigma
macro/condition-specific fail signature
EDS/VM wafer map spatially matches Inline/ET variation
contradicting_evidence: global Vth shift only without sigma/tail increase
VM fail isolated to test condition issue
no ET/Inline proxy movement
recommended_checks: Plot Vmin vs Vth sigma
Check macro and condition split
Compare VM fail map with ET/Inline wafer maps
Separate global shift from tail/sigma issue

similar_cases.title: Short-Lg DIBL/SS excursion after channel release window shift
root_causes: GAA channel release over-etch changed nanosheet width/thickness
similar_cases.outcome: DIBL recovered on corrected release recipe split.

similar_cases.title: Short-Lg DIBL/SS excursion traced to channel release and spacer window
evidence: DIBL and SS increased only on short-Lg ET
Inline channel/spacer proxy shifted by lot_wf
long-Lg Vth did not explain the excursion
similar_cases.resolution: Review channel release and inner spacer process window before concluding WFM root cause.

similar_cases.title: CA contact resistance tail followed CA CD shrink
evidence: Kelvin and chain structures both moved
sheet resistance structure did not move
similar_cases.resolution: Separate sheet/contact structures; review CA lithography/etch and preclean.

similar_cases.title: Contact resistance increased after clean-to-deposition queue delay
evidence: same chamber alone did not separate lots
similar_cases.resolution: Confirm time window ordering and p95/max duration before assigning QTIME root cause.

similar_cases.title: CA contact resistance rise with CA CD shrink
root_causes: CA etch bias and liner thickness increase reduced contact area
evidence: chain tail fail increased at edge wafers
similar_cases.outcome: CA Rc improved after etch bias correction.
